channel.title: HoopsHype Rumors - Phoenix Suns
channel.description: Phoenix Suns trade rumors and free agency buzz from HoopsHype

item.title: Dillon Brooks on LeBron James: I don't think it's his last year, I think he has one more in him
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] “I don’t think it’s his last year,” Suns forward Dillon Brooks, who was in attendance, told Yahoo Sports. “I think he got one more in him. I watched him when I was in the NBA, when I was a kid in high school. He’s got a phenomenal career and battled everybody and done it at a high level throughout. It’s been amazing to watch overall, a pleasure to battle against him.” Yahoo! Sports

item.title: Dillon Brooks courtside and laughs at LeBron having …
item.description: Dillon Brooks courtside and laughs at LeBron having words for OKC after foul 😂

item.title: Some early winners include Michigan’s Aday Mara (7’3″ …
item.description: Prospects went through anthropometric measurements at the draft combine on Monday. The official measurements won’t be finalized until Tuesday, notes Jonathan Givony of DraftExpress (via Twitter), but some early winners include Michigan’s Aday Mara (7’3″ and 260 pounds with a 7’6″ wingspan and a 9’9″ standing reach, tied with Mark Williams for the second-longest in combine history), Jayden Quaintance and Chris Cenac, while Christian Anderson and Amari Allen were among the measurement losers, acco

item.title: Dillon Brooks courtside and laughs at LeBron having …
item.description: Dillon Brooks courtside and laughs at LeBron having words for OKC after foul 😂

item.title: Some early winners include Michigan’s Aday Mara (7’3″ …
item.description: Prospects went through anthropometric measurements at the draft combine on Monday. The official measurements won’t be finalized until Tuesday, notes Jonathan Givony of DraftExpress (via Twitter), but some early winners include Michigan’s Aday Mara (7’3″ and 260 pounds with a 7’6″ wingspan and a 9’9″ standing reach, tied with Mark Williams for the second-longest in combine history), Jayden Quaintance and Chris Cenac, while Christian Anderson and Amari Allen were among the measurement losers, acco

item.title: It’s hard to gauge whether the Rockets got the better …
item.description: How do you think the Kevin Durant trade played out for the Rockets? Was it worth losing players such as Jalen Green and Dillon Brooks? Metta World Peace: I think it’s played out the same way for both the Rockets and the Phoenix Suns. It was quite an even trade. It’s tough for the Rockets because Durant missed Game 1 with a knee injury, then injured his ankle in Game 2, causing him to miss more time. That hurt them a lot. I’ve been injured in the playoffs, so I know how it feels. I was supposed t

item.title: 76ers tried to make trades for Donte DiVincenzo, Naji Marshall, Ryan Dunn, Brandin Podziemski at deadline
item.description: Among other players the source said the Sixers made runs at, unsuccessfully: Naji Marshall of the Dallas Mavericks, Donte DiVincenzo of the Minnesota Timberwolves, Brandin Podziemski of the Golden State Warriors and Ryan Dunn of the Phoenix Suns. None of those players were moved.

item.title: Dillon Brooks: Once Wemby gets his first MVP, you won't see anybody else get one
item.description: Brooks spoke about Victor Wembanyama’s incredible talent, saying that once the French star reaches his peak and wins his first MVP award, we might not see another player claim the honor until he retires. "Once Wemby gets his first, you probably won't see anybody else get one," Brooks said. "His numbers are gonna go up."

item.title: Brooks tells Ray that Victor Wembanyama hasn't reached …
item.description: Slatt♱: Dillon Brooks tells Ray that Victor Wembanyama hasn't reached his pull potential yet & he's the only one in the NBA who can guard him

item.title: Darryn Peterson breaks down what he's learning from Devin Booker, Anthony Edwards, SGA, Donovan Mitchell and Dame Lillard
item.description: Q. Some film you're watching of guys that you're trying to emulate at the next level? Darryn Peterson: Devin Booker, Anthony Edwards, Shai,, Donovan Mitchell, and Dame. Q. What are you taking from them particularly? Peterson: Just ways that they score. They all score in different ways. Let's see how they take space and most of them are getting guarded super hard during their game. How they get easy shots, how they get to free throw line.

item.title: When I spoke to Darryn Peterson he had a very clear …
item.description: Ramona Shelburne: When I spoke to Darryn Peterson he had a very clear answer on what current player he admired: Devin Booker. Joined NBA Today to discuss with @king .mclure @marcjspears @MJAcostaTV #nba #nbatoday

item.title: "I'm going to throw a shot. I can't even lie. This guy …
item.description: Fullcourtpass: Dillon Brooks called Jared Vanderbilt a ‘dumb NBA player’ "I'm going to throw a shot. I can't even lie. This guy doesn't even like me, to be honest with you. Jared Vanderbilt. On the Lakers. They call him Vando” (h/t @PurpGoldLakers)

item.title: Team and league sources told The Athletic that there …
item.description: Two years ago, Connelly was feverishly trying to line up a series of moves to make it possible for the Wolves to land Kevin Durant from Phoenix. Team and league sources told The Athletic that there was a point in the hours before the trade deadline in 2025 that the Wolves believed they were in position to execute, but the Suns ultimately decided not to pull the trigger.

item.title: Western Conference Finals game is going to double …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: A Western Conference Finals game is going to double overtime for the first time since 1976 (Game 4 of Warriors/Suns).

item.title: 11 coaches have made the NBA Finals since 2020
item.description: Tim Reynolds: 11 coaches have made the NBA Finals since 2020. 6 are no longer with the teams they took to those Finals. 2024: Jason Kidd, Dallas 2023: Michael Malone, Denver 2022: Ime Udoka, Boston 2021: Monty Williams, Phoenix 2021: Mike Budenholzer, Milwaukee 2020: Frank Vogel, Lakers

item.title: Nick U’Ren, general manager for the WNBA’s Phoenix …
item.description: Nick U’Ren, general manager for the WNBA’s Phoenix Mercury, is expected to interview for the position, according to league sources. Current Minnesota Timberwolves general manager Matt Lloyd is also expected to interview. Lloyd, league sources say, has the backing of a few key members of the Sixers’ organization. He was a finalist for the Chicago Bulls front-office opening that eventually went to Bryson Graham. Trent Redden, currently the general manager with the LA Clippers, has also emerged as

item.title: McDonald’s has been a longtime fixture in the …
item.description: McDonald’s has been a longtime fixture in the basketball community and culture, now we’re stepping onto the court in a whole new way. Introducing the Nike Book 2 McDonald’s, a limited-edition sneaker designed by Devin Booker with nods to his basketball come-up, and his favorite Sedona *turquoise* Arches – with an exclusive pair available only via Nike Book 2 McDonald’s Friends & Family sweepstakes in the McDonald’s app with purchase of one of the new specialty drinks. The sneakers are a continua

item.title: Dillon Brooks went on social media to promote a new …
item.description: On Thursday, Dillon Brooks went on social media to promote a new board game of his own doing called “Unethical Hoops”, per Underdog. It is a game designed to target SGA and use his likeness. The game is modeled after the surgery-themed board game Operation, in which players use make-shift tweezers to perform “surgery”. Players use the pliers to retrieve miniature basketballs. The board features notable examples of flopping, including “lean-in, “push-off,” and “rip-through”.

item.title: Unethical Hoops, coming soon…
item.description: Underdog: Unethical Hoops, coming soon…

item.title: Robert McCray V from Florida State is another player …
item.description: John Gambadoro: Robert McCray V from Florida State is another player the Suns have had a pre-draft workout with. The 6-4 PG, who played four years of college basketball, is a projected second-round pick.

item.title: Two more names for Phoenix Suns pre-draft workouts are …
item.description: John Gambadoro: Two more names for Phoenix Suns pre-draft workouts are Louisville Guard Ryan Conwell and St. John's forward Bryce Hopkins. Both players will come in next week for workouts.

item.title: Bulls considering Cavs' Johnnie Bryant for their head coaching position
item.description: Furthermore, Cleveland Cavaliers associate head coach Johnnie Bryant is a potential candidate being considered for Chicago’s head coaching position, league sources told HoopsHype. Bryant was a finalist for Phoenix’s head coaching job before Jordan Ott was ultimately hired.

item.title: 76ers assistant general manager Jameer Nelson has also …
item.description: Thus far, external candidates, including Cleveland’s Mike Gansey, Minnesota’s Matt Lloyd, and Phoenix Mercury general manager Nick U'Ren, have emerged. Internally, 76ers assistant general manager Jameer Nelson has also positioned himself well to remain with the organization and be in the mix for a possible promotion, league sources told HoopsHype.

item.title: Overall, Oklahoma City and San Antonio are the only …
item.description: Overall, Oklahoma City and San Antonio are the only other playoff teams with a double-digit positive net rating, and that’s even after they’ve spent the last four games smashing each other’s heads in. The Thunder had a plus-16.9 net rating against the Lakers and plus-18.1 against the Suns; the Spurs were plus-12.9 against Portland and plus-15.9 against Minnesota, even with Victor Wembanyama missing time in both series. Because of that, it’s fair to say that the Knicks should be an underdog in th

item.title: Against Phoenix in the first round, Shai …
item.description: Against Phoenix in the first round, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander fell 11 times on his shot attempts. In the Lakers series, that same number bumped up to 13 falls. With 22 falls in this series, his fall frequency has increased significantly, and he has now fallen twice as often in the Western Conference finals on a per-game basis than he did in the first-round sweep over the Suns.

item.title: Of SGA's 131 points in this series, 49 (37.4%) have …
item.description: Dan Weiss: Of SGA's 131 points in this series, 49 (37.4%) have come from the foul line, far exceeding his percentage in the regular season (25.5%) & even in the first two rounds of the playoffs (31.7%) vs. the Suns & Lakers. Spurs have had success when they keep SGA off the line.

item.title: Underdog refuses to back down after Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's legal team targets foul-drawing board game
item.description: Underdog is refusing to bend after an attorney for Shai Gilgeous-Alexander demanded the company “destroy” copies of a promotional board game poking fun at the NBA MVP’s proclivity for drawing fouls. The sports prediction market and fantasy-sports company made 100 copies of Unethical Hoops, a promotional item based on the classic board game Operation. The game, which was announced via a social media post featuring Suns forward Dillon Brooks, can be won by Underdog users, with winners set to be an

item.title: Other candidates for the Bulls coaching vacancy …
item.description: Jamal Collier: Young was a lead assistant with the Phoenix Suns from 2020-24 before getting hired at BYU. Other candidates for the Bulls coaching vacancy include Tiago Splitter, Micah Nori, Johnnie Bryant, Jerry Stackhouse and Lamar Skeeter

item.title: "I'd say they're a unique team in terms of personnel, …
item.description: "He was a huge [contributor] in ways that may not be in the box score or visible," Thunder coach Mark Daigneault said. "We didn't get outscored by much when he was on the court. I can take some responsibility for [Holmgren's lack of shot attempts], but I still think he played a big-time game in some areas." After making 37 of 38 shots in the restricted area in the first two rounds, Holmgren appeared hesitant to finish in the paint against the 7-foot-4 Wembanyama, the unanimous Defensive Player o

item.title: NBAers in for Canada this summer:
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: To sum up NBAers in for Canada this summer: SGA, NAW, Barrett, Brooks, Dort, Nembhard bros, Mathurin, Olynyk, George, Miller Out this summer but in for this cycle: Edey, Powell Didn’t commit: Murray, Sharpe, Wiggins Committed but not selected: Lawson, Banton, Houstan, Prosper

item.title: Booker tried to say something, he listened to him, and …
item.description: Jusuf Nurkic: “Right after arriving at camp, he was extremely negative toward everyone. It didn’t matter whether it was me, Booker, or anyone else. Every practice was full of criticism — ‘you are not good enough’, ‘you should not be playing basketball’. Nobody really took his words seriously at first. We couldn’t understand that at one point he had lost his compass, that he didn’t care how anyone felt, and that is the most important thing in such a team environment. It was no longer about anythi

item.title: It was absurd. In the end, he had a serious alcohol …
item.description: Jusuf Nurkic: After that meeting, nobody cared anymore about what he was showing. He was explaining to Kevin Durant how to make a basket… explaining to him how to score is like teaching a pilot how to fly a plane. It was absurd. In the end, he had a serious alcohol problem, that is known. Maybe that also affected things, but he completely lost control with the team. He started holding one-on-one meetings just to provoke players — Beal, Allen — you would come there and not believe what he was say

item.title: Tre Donaldson has completed NBA pre-draft workouts …
item.description: Tobias Bass: Miami’s Tre Donaldson has completed NBA pre-draft workouts with the Jazz, Suns, Spurs, Lakers, Warriors, Clippers, Knicks He will also workout with the Hornets, Bucks, Heat, Kings, Raptors, Celtics, Pistons, Magic, Nuggets and Trail Blazers

item.title: “In 1973, we were injured most of the season,” Frazier …
item.description: The Knicks, who beat the Lakers 4-1 in the 1973 NBA Finals for their most recent championship, have the fifth-longest title drought in the NBA behind the Sacramento Kings (1951), Atlanta Hawks (1958), Phoenix Suns (founded in 1968, no titles) and Los Angeles Clippers (founded in 1970, no titles). Frazier said today’s Knicks remind him of the 1973 squad. “In 1973, we were injured most of the season,” Frazier said. “But coming towards the playoffs, we started to get healthy. It reminds me of what

item.title: The Knicks are the third team in NBA Finals history to …
item.description: NBA Communications: The Knicks are the third team in NBA Finals history to win the first two games of a series on the road: 1993 — Bulls (vs. Suns); 1995 — Rockets (vs. Magic); 2026 — Knicks (vs. Spurs)

item.title: “That’s crazy how that turned out,” Fleming said June …
item.description: Rasheer Fleming was preparing for the 2025 NBA Draft at this time last year. He never had a pre-draft workout in Phoenix, but the Suns wound up making multiple draft moves to land him with the first pick of the second round (31st overall). “That’s crazy how that turned out,” Fleming said June 5 at the organization’s youth basketball camp at West Point High in Avondale. “It flew by so fast. I was just talking with BG (Suns general manager) Brian Gregory about that.”

item.title: “That would be good, but I’m really just trying to be …
item.description: Now Fleming is looking to improve his game this summer and compete for a starting spot. “That would be good, but I’m really just trying to be the best version of myself,” Fleming said. “If that ends up getting me to the starting position, that’s what it is.”

item.title: "The defense is going to keep progressing. Just …
item.description: Fleming looks to continue improvement this summer for his second NBA season. “Just keep progressing on my game,” Fleming said. “Overall, just working on different areas of my game. My shot. Handle. Making reads. Slowing down. Just everything overall for me. "The defense is going to keep progressing. Just working on a little bit of everything.”

item.title: This is just the 2nd NBA Finals in which the home team …
item.description: ‪NBA Stat‬: This is just the 2nd NBA Finals in which the home team has lost each of the first 3 games of the series. The 1st such occurrence came in 1993, when the Chicago Bulls opened the Finals with two road wins over the Phoenix Suns before the Suns returned the favor in Game 3. (via @statitudes.com ) #NBA

item.title: Already shot this one down a few weeks back, but a …
item.description: Gerald Bouquet: Seeing the Ja Morant-to-Phoenix talk heating up again. Already shot this one down a few weeks back, but a source reiterated today that the Suns are not interested in trading for Morant

item.title: Milwaukee Bucks will add Vince Legarza & Brad Jones as …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Milwaukee Bucks will add Vince Legarza & Brad Jones as assistant coaches. Legarza was an assistant with Mike Budenholzer on the Suns & Bucks. He & Taylor Jenkins both worked together under Budenholzer on the Hawks. Jones was an assistant for Jenkins on the Grizzlies.

item.title: Rochester to Sacramento Kings: 75 seasons w/o title St …
item.description: ‪Law Murray 📘‬: NBA franchises that have waited 54+ years in the NBA for a title entering the 2026-27 season: Rochester to Sacramento Kings: 75 seasons w/o title St Louis to Atlanta Hawks: 68 seasons w/o title Phoenix Suns: 58 seasons w/o title Buffalo to LA Clippers: 56 seasons w/o title

item.title: Utah Jazz will match any offer sheet Walker Kessler …
item.description: The expectation around the league is that the Utah Jazz will match any offer sheet Walker Kessler receives, and executives hold a similar view regarding Detroit and Jalen Duren. Another restricted free agent center at least worth monitoring is Mark Williams in Phoenix (yeah, the guy the Lakers traded for before rescinding the deal). But it’s only logical to wonder why the Suns would give up assets to acquire Williams only to let him walk a season later.

item.title: Suns already negotiating with Collin Gillespie, Jordan Goodwin
item.description: League sources say Phoenix has opened discussions with two of its own free agents in this early negotiation window: Collin Gillespie and Jordan Goodwin.

item.title: Arizona is f*cking hot, man. It's hot than a …
item.description: Leandro Barbosa: "When I first came to the Phoenix Suns, I thought I was going to live the same way I live in Brazil. I used to go to practice riding my bike, doing my things, like regular, we simple. But Arizona is f*cking hot, man. It's hot than a motherf*cker. So I couldn't done that, you know? I did it a couple times, but, you know, so the transition for me to understand, you know, like the two practice a day, you know, like how many hours we will be practicing on the training camp, you know

item.title: Devin Booker changing from No. 1 to No. 15, to honor his father, and for Team USA number
item.description: Shams Charania: Phoenix Suns star Devin Booker is changing his jersey from No. 1 to No. 15, honoring his USA Basketball uniform and the college number of his father, Melvin, per ESPN sources.

item.title: Sources say Phoenix is talking trades that could net a …
item.description: Sources say Phoenix is talking trades that could net a first-round pick in this draft. The Suns are currently only guaranteed to make one pick next week: No. 47 in Wednesday's second round.

item.title: Suns tender qualifying offers to Mark Williams, Koby Brea
item.description: Keith Smith: The Phoenix Suns have tendered qualifying offers to Mark Williams and Koby Brea, a league source told @spotrac. Williams and Brea will now be restricted free agents this offseason.

item.title: Williams' qualifying offer is for $9.6M. He reached …
item.description: Keith Smith: Mark Williams' qualifying offer is for $9.6M. He reached starter criteria, which triggered a higher qualifying offer. Brea's qualifying offer is for another two-way contract.

item.title: Pelicans have hired AJ Meyer as Director of Analytics
item.description: Shamit Dua: Pelicans have hired AJ Meyer as Director of Analytics. Meyer comes from the Suns with previous stints in Dallas and San Antonio.

item.title: Like, I got to know Kobe, you know what I mean? And …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Matt Barnes on his relationship with Kobe Bryant: “I remember when the Drake and Rick Ross song came out Stay Schemin' and you wasn’t with me shooting in the gym. I remember when that sh*t came out and how made he was about that was crazy and I’m going kind to leave that where that was. But then I also remember another time where we just having a good time. We were in Phoenix on the bus about to get on the plane and we were joking talking sh*t because I came here off the rip just

item.title: Collin Gillespie’s four-year, $48 million deal to …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Collin Gillespie’s four-year, $48 million deal to return to Phoenix is fully guaranteed, sources say. Quite the well-deserved payday for the Villanova product after thriving for the Suns on a minimum salary last season.

item.title: By my projections Suns will be roughly $4.6M under the …
item.description: Keith Smith: By my projections Suns will be roughly $4.6M under the tax and $12.6M under the first apron after re-signing Collin Gillespie. Can create more room if they move on from Haywood Highsmith and/or Jamaree Bouyea. It'll get tight to avoid the first apron if they re-sign Mark Williams and Jordan Goodwin.

item.title: Collin Gillespie agrees to four-year, $48 million deal to return to Phoenix
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent guard Collin Gillespie intends to sign a four-year, $48 million deal to return to the Phoenix Suns, sources tell ESPN. Gillespie spent three straight seasons on two-way contracts after going undrafted out of Villanova in 2022 and now earns a lucrative long-term deal.

item.title: Gillespie’s agents at Excel Sports Management, Jordan …
item.description: Shams Charania: Gillespie’s agents at Excel Sports Management, Jordan Gertler and James Dunleavy, negotiated a deal with Suns executives after teams became eligible to talk to their own free agents last weekend. The sides came together on a deal for a guard who has emerged as a significant member and success story of the team's new culture around grit and toughness. Last season, Gillespie had a breakout campaign setting a Suns franchise record for 3-pointers with 232 and averaging career-highs i

item.title: Dillon Brooks extension could be worth $25 million per season
item.description: In his first season with Phoenix, Brooks remained a strong defender, helped change the team’s culture, and became a 20-point-per-game scorer for the first time in his career. At 30, Brooks enters the upcoming season on the books for $21 million. Looking ahead, Brooks could earn a potential extension around an average of $25 million per season.

item.title: Lastly, the Suns have also done some background on …
item.description: Lastly, the Suns have also done some background on Chicago Bulls guard Josh Giddey, HoopsHype has learned.

item.title: They are committed to bringing back their own free …
item.description: John Gambadoro: YES the Suns can afford both. They are committed to bringing back their own free agents and willing to go deep into the luxury tax to do that. They won't go into the second apron, but they shouldn't have to.

item.title: Love Island USA Season 8 has started, with audiences …
item.description: Love Island USA Season 8 has started, with audiences getting to know fresh faces in the Fiji setting — and the astute might recognize one name. Aniya Harvey is the daughter of retired NBA star Donnell Harvey, who played for the Dallas Mavericks, Orlando Magic, New Jersey Nets, Denver Nuggets and Phoenix Suns. Harvey made his final NBA appearance in 2005 but continued to play professionally internationally until 2014. According to Complex (1), Harvey has largely been out of the spotlight since he

item.title: Jordan Goodwin to return to Phoenix on three-year, $19 million deal
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent guard Jordan Goodwin intends to sign a three-year, $19 million deal to return to the Phoenix Suns, with a player option in the third season, sources tell ESPN. Goodwin made the Suns roster out of training camp on a non-guaranteed contract and emerged as a key reserve and culture fit, averaging 8.7 points, 4.9 rebounds, 2.2 assists, and a team-high 106 total steals.

item.title: In consecutive days, the Suns land multiyear …
item.description: Shams Charania: In consecutive days, the Suns land multiyear agreements to keep two young guards in Goodwin and Collin Gillespie whom the franchise developed over the last year. Goodwin's agents at Klutch Sports, Shy Saee and Mike George, landed the deal Sunday, a week after free agents became eligible to negotiate with their own teams. Next order of business for the Suns: retaining restricted free agent Mark Williams.

item.title: Lewis also completed workouts this past week with the …
item.description: Lewis also completed workouts this past week with the Phoenix Suns, Dallas Mavericks, Minnesota Timberwolves, Boston Celtics, and Cleveland Cavaliers, sources said, ahead of the NBA Draft, which takes place over June 24 and 25 (AEDT) at the Barclays Centre in Brooklyn, New York.

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo trade may expand before July 6
item.description: The trade does not include any additional teams -- it is a one-to-one move, sources said -- but both the Heat and Bucks will execute the deal on July 6, which leaves the framework open to see if there are opportunities to expand. Last June, the Phoenix Suns and Houston Rockets agreed to a deal that ultimately increased to a historic seven-team trade.

item.title: Here are some names I expect the Phoenix Suns to have …
item.description: John Gambadoro: Ok I know Suns fans have been waiting for this - Here are some names I expect the Phoenix Suns to have interest in if they stay at #47 or move up in the second round. Not sure they can get any of these at 47 so main reason I have been saying I expect they attempt to move up in the second-round. Baba Miller Trevon Brazile Tyler Bilodeau Ja'Kobi Gillespie Joshua Jefferson Dillon Mitchell

item.title: Phoenix moves up to No. 30 to take Koa Peat via Dallas
item.description: Brett Siegel: This trade is done, sources told @ClutchPoints . The Suns are moving up to No. 30 from Dallas to take Koa Peat.

item.title: Knicks now hold picks 31, 47 (via Suns) and 55 in …
item.description: Marc Stein: The Knicks now hold picks 31, 47 (via Suns) and 55 in Wednesday's Round 2 and will add four more future second-rounders when the moves made tonight are official. Dallas and Phoenix say they can't discuss No. 25 Sergio De Larrea and No. 30 Koa Peat until the moves are finalized.

item.title: The Suns did NOT workout Koa Peat because of where …
item.description: John Gambadoro: Interesting nugget here. The Suns did NOT workout Koa Peat because of where they were picking in the second round. But once they felt like they had a chance - albeit a small one to get into the first round - they were able to get him to come into the building on Sunday for a visit.

item.title: I’m told that the many moving parts that landed Cam …
item.description: Marc Stein: I’m told that the many moving parts that landed Cam Carr with the Lakers at No. 24, Sergio De Larrea with the Mavericks at No. 25, Koa Peat with the Suns at No. 30 and netted the Knicks five second-round picks is tracking to take the form of a four-team TRADE once made official.
